objective: Harden Launch-Gate Lifecycle and Add Reusable Log Regression Analysis
red_evidence: Each feature implementation phase established failing RED tests showing launch-gate gaps (cold state skipping, watchdog resuming over conflicts, raw payload diagnostics).
design_decisions: Introduced explicit tracking readiness states (cold, ready, failed) to decouple QAM UI selection from lifecycle initialization.
Enforced conservative cold-state gating by treating a game launch without tracking data as tracked to prevent skipped gate, canceling the speculative watch on backend rejection.
Replaced inference-based watchdog timeouts with a renewable pause lease system to guarantee safe pause during frontend conflict wait scenarios.
Changed Syncthing watch ownership across phases to aggressively supersede prior watches to avoid race conditions during state transitions.
Added bounds to diagnostic payloads using summary helpers and bounded object descriptions to avoid huge stringifications.
results: All tests passed with GREEN output. The launch gate correctly hydrates tracking, pauses cold starts conservatively, maintains pauses with renewable leases, supersedes cross-phase Syncthing watches cleanly, and sanitizes logs effectively.
